summary: Ship the per-genre display rule — a formatter rendering a `GenreValue` (leaves + collections) to projection markdown: headings, Chosen/Rejected labels, ordering, anchors — connective tissue only

new.description: The per-genre display rule: `fn render_genre(genre: &GenreValue) -> String` over the extracted value (`src/extract.rs` `GenreValue` — `genre`/`key` identity, `leaves` BTreeMap, `collections`). Emits the fenced block's projection markdown — headings, the Chosen/Rejected labels, deterministic ordering (BTreeMap key order; leaves before collections), anchors — as CONNECTIVE TISSUE ONLY: every meaning-carrying word traces to an authored leaf or a declared value's rendered form (reuse the mention-resolution pattern in `src/read.rs` `LeafAddress`, `specs/architecture/45-governance.md`). No synthesized prose. Byte-deterministic; a double-render of the same value must be byte-identical (law 5). Standalone formatter island — the future custom-kind emit face calls it; it does NOT wire into `src/drift.rs`'s skill/rule projection (that projection has no genre carriage today).

Pin the display rule's byte contract with insta snapshots: a Decision-genre value (Chosen/Rejected leaves + a keyed collection) renders to its exact markdown; leaves-only and collections-only values render; ordering is deterministic across two renders (double-render byte-identical); an empty genre renders its heading with no body. The snapshot IS the pinned contract the future emit face must reproduce.

edit.description: Add `pub mod display;` in module order (after `pub mod coverage_note;`, before `pub mod document;`). Foundation line for the new module — this entry owns the `lib.rs` edit outright.

tests.asserts: a genre value renders to its pinned projection markdown (headings, Chosen/Rejected labels, ordered leaves then keyed collections, anchors); every rendered word traces to a leaf; a double-render is byte-identical; an empty genre renders heading-only.

acceptance: `render_genre` compiles a `GenreValue` to byte-deterministic projection markdown (connective tissue only, double-render verified), snapshot-pinned; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green. Standalone island — no drift.rs projection wiring (accepted island risk: the future custom-kind emit face reproduces these bytes).
notes: Inbox 07-03: `(display-rule-emit-face)` RESOLVED narrow slice (John) — ships its own engine slice, not riding the custom-kind emit face. Disjoint from the sdk/ entries (src/ only; lib.rs owned here). Emit projects only skill/rule bodies today (drift.rs:459-477), so the slice delivers the spec-pinned formatter as an island; the custom-kind emit face absorbs it later. Unblocks the genre-adoption pilot (a human ceremony, not an entry).

summary: Resolve member bodies before emit: `fromFile` asset resolution and opt-in mention resolution-checking — the two scaffold bounds `bodyText` throws on today

per.section: Mentions — prose that declares its own references

edit.description: `bodyText` (emit.ts:369) throws on both `fromFile` and any mention today (the scaffold's stated bounds). Resolve them: `fromFile` reads the referenced asset into the body before serialize; mentions are resolution-checked one-way, obligation-free, opt-in per word (20-surface, `a mention is opt-in per word — no completeness check, ever`; law 8's never-climb bound) — an unresolved mention is a loud emit error, never a silent pass. No prose grep; only declared mention edges.

Thread the resolved body/mention set through the `Member` shape so `emit.ts` and `toManifestMember` consume resolved bodies. Keep the fromFile vs inline body union; resolution happens at emit, not at authoring.
Extend with fromFile-resolved bodies and mention-resolution cases (resolved mention renders; unresolved mention fails loud).

tests.asserts: a `fromFile` body resolves and emits; a resolved mention renders to its declared value's form; an unresolved mention is a loud emit error; emit stays byte-deterministic.

acceptance: SDK emit resolves `fromFile` bodies and resolution-checks mentions (loud on unresolved), staying byte-parity with the Rust emitter; `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Un-blocked 07-03: SDK-EMIT-BYTE-PARITY shipped (build 272b4f4 / chore 0dd318a; verified — emit.ts byte-parity in place, the two `bodyText` bounds at emit.ts:369-383 still throw). Combines the scaffold's `fromFile` + `mention` bounds (both edit `bodyText`) into one slice. SDK-PROJECTION-LOCK stays blockedBy this over the shared emit.ts.

summary: SDK emit writes the harness projection (members → `.claude/**`) and stamps the lock's freshness fingerprints — the last two scaffold bounds, both deliberately absent today

new.description: Projection writing: compile each member to its harness format under `.claude/**` (the projection half of emit), whole-file re-emit, byte-faithful. Mirrors the Rust emit projector; the emit orchestrator calls it after the manifest is serialized.

Lock stamping: compute and write the `source_hash`/`emit_hash` freshness fingerprints the drift engine reads (`config.stale`), matching the Rust lock's hash inputs so an SDK-emitted lock and a Rust-emitted lock agree for the same face.

edit.description: Export `project`/`lock` and wire them into the emit entry so a full `emit` compiles manifest + projection + lock in one deterministic pass.
Cover projection byte-fidelity and lock-fingerprint agreement with the Rust emitter for a shared fixture.

tests.asserts: SDK projection output matches the Rust projector byte-for-byte for a shared member, and the SDK-stamped lock's `source_hash`/`emit_hash` match the Rust lock for the same face.

acceptance: A full SDK `emit` writes the manifest, the `.claude/**` projection, and a lock whose fingerprints agree with the Rust emitter; `pnpm --dir sdk test` green. (Blocked behind SDK-BODY-RESOLUTION.)
notes: Closes the scaffold's remaining bounds (projection writing, lock stamping). New modules (project.ts/lock.ts) plus index.ts wiring; blockedBy SDK-BODY-RESOLUTION for the shared emit orchestration. Last link of the altitude authoring wave.

summary: Make `Primitive::Field` honor the nested key-path its doc-comment promises — held deferred until a nested-key kind (a JSON-manifest kind) consumes it

per.section: The extraction algebra — the soundness boundary, as data

gate.reason: no consumer — the Fenced half graduated to FENCED-PRIMITIVE (its genre-fence consumer arrived); the remaining key-path half has none. `Primitive::Field` reads a flat `fields.get(key)`, so a nested `a.b.c` path can't be read — but no current kind (skill/rule/memory) declares a nested key-path field. Revive when a kind demands it (a JSON-manifest kind's nested key-path field).

edit.description: Make `Primitive::Field` honor the key-path its own doc-comment promises: the impl does a flat `unit.frontmatter.get(key)` in `apply`, so a nested `a.b.c` path can't be read. No new vocabulary member — this fixes the existing `field` primitive.

Key-path traversal for the field extractor: walk nested TOML/JSON tables to the leaf before `json_to_feature`, kind-preserving per the `type` lattice (`specs/architecture/10-contracts.md`). Absent (not errored) when the path doesn't resolve.

tests.asserts: a key-path `field` reads a nested value and is absent (not errored) when the path doesn't resolve; it preserves the source scalar kind.

acceptance: A nested-key-path `Primitive::Field` reads `a.b.c` over a `Unit` and is absent when unresolved; a KIND.md declaring it reads the intended feature; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green. (Deferred: not built until a kind consumes it.)
notes: Narrowed 07-03: the Fenced half graduated to FENCED-PRIMITIVE (consumer arrived). Only the key-path `field` half remains, still no consumer. `Primitive::Field { key }` still flat-reads on disk (kind.rs). Shares kind.rs/extract.rs with AGENT-KIND — serialize the two if both un-defer.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` as a member document, read through the generic loader and checked by the embedded `agent.anthropic` package

per.section: Decision: package binding is by artifact kind

gate.reason: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; and post-adapter-wave the agent kind is mostly curated data (a KIND.md + PACKAGE.md, both outside build's fence) with near-zero engine code — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The embedded built-in `agent` KIND.md (curated — humans author it; outside build's fence), under its provider beside kinds/claude-code/skill and kinds/claude-code/rule with a `provider = "claude-code"` line: `governs = { root = '.claude/agents', glob = '*.md' }`, the declared `yaml-frontmatter` projection format + unit shape, composed extraction over the `name`/`description`/optional `tools`/`model` fields plus markdown structure. build.rs auto-embeds it — no engine extractor.

The agent kind's built-in package `agent.anthropic` (curated product territory, outside build's fence), embedded with the std-lib by build.rs. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`. Per-clause `source` citations to the Claude Code subagent docs.

Fixture-driven proof mirroring tests/rule_contract.rs: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an import round-trip insta snapshot proving idempotence — all through the generic adapter + loader, no per-kind engine code.

edit.description: Add `"agent"` to the `BUILTIN_KINDS` name-list so a `[kind.agent]` binding reads as a built-in layer, not a custom kind.
Add the `AGENT_PACKAGE = "agent.anthropic"` const beside SKILL_PACKAGE/RULE_PACKAGE and register its kind->package binding. build.rs auto-embeds the PACKAGE.md; no manual embed edit.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the `agent.anthropic` package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently via the generic frontmatter adapter; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Declared-adapter architecture: no src/agent.rs IR — the generic adapter projects it. Curated kinds/claude-code/agent/KIND.md + packages/agent.anthropic/PACKAGE.md sit outside build's fence; engine wiring is near-zero. Stays deferred: wrong direction under requirements/satisfies. Shares src/kind.rs with EXTRACTION-VOCAB-GAPS — serialize the two if both un-defer.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from the existing `.github/workflows/temper.yml` CI job.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/architecture/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (the authoring-face manifest).

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB root `package.json` is the *private* flume manifest (≠ sdk/package.json) — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Only `.github/workflows/temper.yml` (CI) exists — no release.yml yet. Parked until a human sets creds up.
